expectations.text: A Node class is defined with attributes that include at least coordinates, an ID, and a demand or area field named in domain terms (demand_ton, area_sqft, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Node has fac_id, county_fips, lon, lat, area_sqft, demand_ton_2025, demand_ton_2030 — all domain-meaningful names matching project conventions

expectations.text: A NetworkManager or equivalent class is defined that contains a collection of nodes and links
expectations.evidence: NetworkManager has self.nodes: list[Node] and self.links: list[Link], with add_node() and add_link() methods

expectations.text: No abstract base classes or complex inheritance — flat OOP structure
expectations.evidence: Node and Link are @dataclass, NetworkManager is a plain class. No ABC, no inheritance chain

expectations.text: Attributes use domain-meaningful names, not single-letter variables like 'x', 'd', 'n'
expectations.evidence: demand_ton_2025, area_sqft, travel_time_h, distance_km, highway_corridor, near_interstate, near_rail — all explicit domain terms

expectations.text: Each class has a docstring describing the physical entity it represents
expectations.evidence: Node: 'A candidate regional hub facility in the freight network. Physical grounding...'; Link: 'A directed freight corridor...'; NetworkManager: 'Container and operator for the full regional hub network. Physical grounding...'
